steps.title: Find the time signal
steps.content: The phrase "in a later chapter" anchors the action in the future, so the verb must express future time.

steps.title: Eliminate past and present options, confirm the future
steps.content:
• Choice A — had learned: Past perfect — places the action before another past event. No future meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — had been learning: Past perfect progressive — describes an ongoing action that ended in the past. Still no future meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — will learn: Simple future — correctly matches "in a later chapter," placing the action ahead of the present moment. ✓
  • Choice D — have learned: Present perfect — links a past action to the present, not the future. ✗
C is correct.
